sessions.lever: remove $2/$3 register-asm pins; reuse one local 't' for the 3 tail loads + mask-first store

reviewer.evidence: Candidate has ZERO regfix/asmfix rules and ZERO cheat-asm (both forbidden register-asm pins removed). The reused 't' is a genuine value-holding local; mask is a plain const store. Not byte-identical (floor 6) so NOT committed to src/ — clean WIP only.

remaining_gap.summary: Tail control flow + the 3 arg0[i] coord loads now match (all reuse one reg). Residual 6 diffs are purely the 0xFF8080 mask: target builds it in $v1 (lui $v1 early, ori $v1+sw later) INTERLEAVED into the loads' delay slots, keeping the const live in $v1 across the loads. My build hoists the whole lui+ori+sw mask ahead of the loads (in $v0). No C statement ordering controls this intra-basic-block scheduler choice.
remaining_gap.rules_required_for_full_match: HEAD reached byte-match only via 2 cheat-asm pins (register s32 t asm("$2"), register s32 mask asm("$3")) — both forbidden. No legal rule/source form found that reproduces the mask-in-$v1 interleave.

next_hypotheses: decomp-permuter scheduling search from this candidate base (residual is pure scheduler slot-fill of the lui/ori mask split — the permuter's specialty). Verify any find with engine sandbox + retire (faithful), not the isolated permuter score.
If permuter plateaus too, this is a genuine scheduler-coupling -> escalate for canonical-asm authorization (the 2-pin form is its only known byte-match) or accept as a documented plateau.

rejected_forms.form: register s32 t asm("$2"); register s32 mask asm("$3"); (HEAD's committed form)
rejected_forms.reason: FORBIDDEN cheat-asm register-asm pins — see .claude/rules/register-asm-pins / register-alloc-pure-c Lever D and inline-asm-policy. Byte-matches but is not COMPLETED-C.

rejected_forms.form: mask stored mid (between arg0[1] and arg0[2]) — any of: plain const, separate var m, m live across loads
rejected_forms.reason: Not a cheat, just worse: floor 9. Mask-FIRST (floor 6) is strictly better. Do not re-derive the mid-store variants.

rejected_forms.form: three separate load locals x0/x1/x2 (no reuse), mask mid
rejected_forms.reason: Not a cheat, worse (floor 11). Separate locals let GCC hoist all 3 loads into distinct regs — the opposite of target. Single reused 't' is required.
